the lord of the 4th and aspect from Jupiter has a special
significance in this respect. The Amsa indications are also
favourable in this matter.
Paternal Property.—We have found that the 4th house
is particularly strong. There are no contrary indications for
this. So the native can expect to inherit substantial paternal
property as well.
Children.— In Chart No. 125 the 5th house falls in a
fruitful sign namely Pisces, but its lord is in the 6th a
dusthana. To compensate for this as it were, the lord of the
5th house who is also the Putrakaraka receives the aspect of
two natural benefics, Mercury and Venus. Mercury is,
however, lord of 8 and 11 and hence evil for the horoscope.
The 5th house though untenanted by any

planet, benefic or malefic, receives the aspect of a malefic
namely the Sun, who, by being the owner of a kendra has
become a complete benefic. Thus a sort of balance
